484	def normalize_domain(hint: str | None) -> str:
485	    """Map a free-form domain hint to ``{clinical, nonclinical}`` else ``UNKNOWN``.
486	
487	    UNKNOWN is the fail-closed default (design §4.2): an unrecognised / unset /
488	    empty hint dispatches to NO spec -> forced singleton, NEVER a coarse default.
489	    """
490	    s = (str(hint or "")).strip().lower()
491	    if not s:
492	        return _DOMAIN_UNKNOWN
493	    if s in (_DOMAIN_CLINICAL, _DOMAIN_NONCLINICAL):
494	        return s
495	    if s in _CLINICAL_DOMAIN_HINTS:
496	        return _DOMAIN_CLINICAL
497	    if s in _NONCLINICAL_DOMAIN_HINTS:
498	        return _DOMAIN_NONCLINICAL
499	    return _DOMAIN_UNKNOWN

504	    exact float); all others are already strip+lower normalized by their getter."""

508	def build_merge_key(claim: Any) -> tuple:
509	    """Spec-generated, FAIL-CLOSED merge key for one atomic claim (design §4.2).
510	
511	    ``claim`` is a claim view carrying ``kind`` / ``domain`` / ``evidence_id`` /
512	    ``atom_uid`` plus the extractor discriminator fields the slots read.
513	
514	    FAIL-CLOSED on two axes (the clinical-lethal direction is OVER-merge, so every
515	    ambiguity resolves to a SINGLETON):
516	      1. DISPATCH: any ``(kind, domain)`` with no spec — incl. ``kind=='raw'``, a
517	         None/unnormalizable domain, or any uncatalogued pair — returns a globally
518	         unique singleton ``('__unresolved__', kind, str(domain), evidence_id,
519	         atom_uid)``. NEVER a coarse/default spec (silent over-merge).
520	      2. PER-SLOT: any DISCRIMINATOR not positively known (its ``unknown_predicate``
521	         is True) returns the SAME singleton. A defaulted/derived value is unknown
522	         (design §4.3).
523	    Otherwise the tuple is emitted FROM the spec (field-in-key == field-in-spec).
524	
525	    P1-A13 (I-arch-007) RESIDUAL: this fail-closed-on-ANY-unknown rule is WHY clinical
526	    numeric baskets stay singletons (the extractor leaves dose/comparator/effect_measure/
527	    endpoint_phrase/route_formulation blank, each forcing ``__unresolved__``). Letting a
528	    BLANK optional consolidate (ABSENT==ABSENT) would close A13 for clinical numerics, but
529	    that change DIRECTLY violates the Codex-hardened arch002 contracts
530	    (test_20_unknown_discriminator_forces_singleton_even_with_spec / test_5 / test_23 assert
531	    a blank ``comparator``/``direction`` MUST singleton) and the defaulted-arm Slice-B P0 — a
532	    relaxation NOT in this campaign's scope; it needs its own Codex gate. A13 is CLOSED for the
533	    A20-activated path (multi-member clustering IS the default once the redesign flag is on —
534	    fully-populated specs, incl. nonclinical numerics, DO consolidate; smoke-proven) and a
535	    documented residual for the blank-clinical-qualifier case.
536	    """
537	    raw_domain = getattr(claim, "domain", "")
538	    domain = normalize_domain(raw_domain)
539	    kind = str(getattr(claim, "kind", "") or "")
540	    evidence_id = str(getattr(claim, "evidence_id", "") or "")
541	    atom_uid = str(getattr(claim, "atom_uid", "") or "")
542	    spec = MERGE_KEY_SPEC.get((kind, domain))
543	    if spec is None:
544	        # raw / unknown-domain / any uncatalogued (kind, domain) -> forced singleton.
545	        return ("__unresolved__", kind, str(raw_domain), evidence_id, atom_uid)
546	    parts: list[Any] = []
547	    for slot in spec:
548	        v = slot.value_getter(claim)
549	        if slot.role == _ROLE_DISCRIMINATOR and slot.unknown_predicate(v):
550	            return ("__unresolved__", kind, domain, evidence_id, atom_uid)
551	        parts.append(_canonicalize(slot, v))
552	    return tuple(parts)
553	
554	
555	def _merge_key_view(extractor_claim: Any, *, kind: str, evidence_id: str,
556	                    domain: str, atom_uid: str) -> Any:
557	    """A lightweight claim view that exposes the extractor object's discriminator
558	    fields PLUS ``kind`` / ``domain`` / ``evidence_id`` / ``atom_uid`` so a single
559	    ``build_merge_key(view)`` call reads everything it needs.
560	
561	    We wrap (not mutate) the extractor object so its real dataclass is untouched;
562	    ``__getattr__`` delegates any field the spec slots request to the underlying
563	    extractor object (test #21 spec<->extractor binding holds against the real
564	    dataclass fields).
565	    """
566	    return _MergeKeyView(extractor_claim, kind, evidence_id, domain, atom_uid)
567	
568	
569	class _MergeKeyView:
570	    """Read-only view over an extractor claim for ``build_merge_key`` (see above)."""
571	
572	    __slots__ = ("_inner", "kind", "evidence_id", "domain", "atom_uid")
573	
574	    def __init__(self, inner: Any, kind: str, evidence_id: str,
575	                 domain: str, atom_uid: str) -> None:
576	        self._inner = inner
577	        self.kind = kind
578	        self.evidence_id = evidence_id
579	        self.domain = domain
580	        self.atom_uid = atom_uid
581	
582	    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
583	        # only reached for names NOT in __slots__ (i.e. the extractor's own fields)
584	        return getattr(self._inner, name, "")
585	
586	
587	def _row_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
588	    return str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or row.get("text") or "")
589	
590	
591	def _atom_uid(kind: str, evidence_id: str, claim_index: int) -> str:
592	    """Per-atom-unique id (design §4.2 / §9.7, test #23): ``kind:evidence_id:index``.
593	
594	    Two distinct atoms from one ``evidence_id`` (the numeric fan-out produces these)
595	    get DIFFERENT indices, so the fail-closed singleton key cannot collide them.
596	    Deterministic (no uuid/random/time) so the cluster id stays reproducible.
597	    """
598	    return f"{kind}:{evidence_id}:{claim_index}"
599	
600	
601	def extract_atomic_claims(
602	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
603	    *,
604	    domain: str | None = None,
605	    numeric_extractor: Callable[..., list[ExtractedNumericClaim]] = extract_numeric_claims,
606	    qualitative_extractor: Callable[..., list[QualitativeAssertion]] = extract_qualitative_assertions,
607	) -> list[AtomicClaim]:
608	    """Field-agnostic atomic-claim extraction over evidence ``rows``.
609	
610	    Composes the injected numeric + qualitative extractors and ALWAYS falls back to a
611	    conservative raw-text claim, so EVERY non-empty row yields >=1 atomic claim and
612	    nothing is silently dropped. The extractors are dependency-injected (real
613	    defaults; tests pass fakes / fixtures) — keeping this function pure + offline.
614	
615	    Args:
616	        rows: evidence rows (each a dict with at least ``evidence_id`` and a text
617	            field — ``direct_quote`` / ``statement`` / ``text``).
618	        domain: optional domain hint forwarded to the numeric extractor (it routes to
619	            a broader predicate set for non-clinical queries).
620	        numeric_extractor: ``(rows, domain) -> list[ExtractedNumericClaim]``.
621	        qualitative_extractor: ``(rows, domain) -> list[QualitativeAssertion]``.
622	
623	    Returns:
624	        ``list[AtomicClaim]`` with ``claim_cluster_id`` still "" (assigned by
625	        ``cluster_equivalent_claims``). A row that yields a structured claim does NOT
626	        additionally yield a raw claim (avoid double-counting); a row that yields NO
627	        structured claim yields exactly one raw singleton claim.
628	    """
629	    rows = list(rows or [])
630	    out: list[AtomicClaim] = []
631	    structured_evidence_ids: set[str] = set()
632	    # Read the master flag ONCE per call (never import-time). OFF -> the legacy
633	    # positional _normalized_key_* keys run, so the SHA-1 grouping input — and thus
634	    # the whole graph — is byte-identical to the pre-change tree. ON -> the
635	    # spec-driven, fail-closed build_merge_key keys run (design §4).
636	    redesign_on = _credibility_redesign_enabled()
637	    # Normalized domain stamped on each claim (design §4.2 / P3.1). '' on the OFF
638	    # path is inert (never read); on the ON path normalize_domain maps it.
639	    claim_domain = domain or ""
640	
641	    # 1. numeric extractor (per-row so we can attribute claim ids deterministically).
642	    for row in rows:
643	        evid = str(row.get("evidence_id", ""))
644	        try:
645	            numeric_claims = numeric_extractor([row], domain) if domain is not None \
646	                else numeric_extractor([row])
647	        except TypeError:
648	            # an injected extractor that does not accept a domain kwarg
649	            numeric_claims = numeric_extractor([row])
650	        for ci, nc in enumerate(numeric_claims):
651	            structured_evidence_ids.add(evid)
652	            uid = _atom_uid("numeric", evid, ci)
653	            if redesign_on:
654	                nkey = build_merge_key(_merge_key_view(
655	                    nc, kind="numeric", evidence_id=evid,
656	                    domain=claim_domain, atom_uid=uid))
657	            else:
658	                nkey = _normalized_key_numeric(nc, evid, ci)
659	            out.append(AtomicClaim(
660	                evidence_id=evid,
661	                kind="numeric",
662	                subject=(getattr(nc, "subject", "") or "").strip().lower(),
663	                predicate=(getattr(nc, "predicate", "") or "").strip().lower(),
664	                normalized_key=nkey,
665	                text=str(getattr(nc, "context_snippet", "") or _row_text(row))[:200],
666	                source_url=str(getattr(nc, "source_url", "") or row.get("source_url", "")),
667	                source_tier=str(getattr(nc, "source_tier", "") or row.get("tier", "")),
668	                domain=claim_domain,
669	                atom_uid=uid,
670	            ))
671	
672	    # 2. qualitative extractor (whole-corpus; one row may yield several assertions).
673	    try:
674	        qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows, domain) if domain is not None \
675	            else qualitative_extractor(rows)
676	    except TypeError:
677	        qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows)
678	    # group qualitative assertions by evidence_id so the per-claim sentinel index is
679	    # stable + distinct per row.
680	    qual_index_by_evid: dict[str, int] = {}
681	    for qa in qual_assertions:
682	        evid = str(getattr(qa, "evidence_id", "") or "")
683	        ci = qual_index_by_evid.get(evid, 0)
684	        qual_index_by_evid[evid] = ci + 1
685	        structured_evidence_ids.add(evid)
686	        uid = _atom_uid("qualitative", evid, ci)
687	        if redesign_on:
688	            nkey = build_merge_key(_merge_key_view(
689	                qa, kind="qualitative", evidence_id=evid,
690	                domain=claim_domain, atom_uid=uid))
691	        else:
692	            nkey = _normalized_key_qualitative(qa, evid, ci)
693	        out.append(AtomicClaim(
694	            evidence_id=evid,
695	            kind="qualitative",
696	            subject=(getattr(qa, "subject", "") or "").strip().lower(),
697	            predicate=(getattr(qa, "concept_type", "") or "").strip().lower(),
698	            normalized_key=nkey,
699	            text=str(getattr(qa, "context_snippet", "") or "")[:200],
700	            source_url=str(getattr(qa, "source_url", "") or ""),
701	            source_tier=str(getattr(qa, "source_tier", "") or ""),
702	            domain=claim_domain,
703	            atom_uid=uid,
704	        ))
705	
706	    # 3. conservative raw fallback: any row that produced NO structured claim yields
707	    #    exactly one raw singleton, keyed by (evidence_id, normalized text), so it is
708	    #    NEVER merged and NEVER dropped. Field-agnostic coverage guarantee.
709	    for row in rows:
710	        evid = str(row.get("evidence_id", ""))
711	        text = _row_text(row)
712	        if not text.strip():
713	            continue
714	        if evid in structured_evidence_ids:
715	            continue
716	        norm_text = _norm_text_key(text)
717	        # ATOM_UID-RAW-SITE (design §9.7): the raw site has no threaded claim_index.
718	        # Retain the normalized text in atom_uid so even on a duplicate/empty
719	        # evidence_id two raw atoms cannot collide on the fail-closed singleton key
720	        # (build_merge_key returns a singleton for kind=='raw'). On the OFF path the
721	        # key is byte-identical to today's ("__raw__", evid, norm_text).
722	        if redesign_on:
723	            uid = f"raw:{evid}:{norm_text}"
724	            nkey = build_merge_key(_merge_key_view(
725	                row, kind="raw", evidence_id=evid,
726	                domain=claim_domain, atom_uid=uid))
727	        else:
728	            uid = ""
729	            nkey = ("__raw__", evid, norm_text)
730	        out.append(AtomicClaim(
731	            evidence_id=evid,
732	            kind="raw",
733	            subject="",
734	            predicate="",
735	            normalized_key=nkey,
736	            text=text[:200],
737	            source_url=str(row.get("source_url", "")),
738	            source_tier=str(row.get("tier", "")),
739	            domain=claim_domain,
740	            atom_uid=uid,
741	        ))
742	
743	    return out
744	
745	
746	def _claim_cluster_id(normalized_key: tuple) -> str:
747	    """Deterministic, stable id for an equivalence cluster.
748	
749	    SHA-1 over a canonical string rendering of the normalized key, truncated +
750	    prefixed. Deterministic (no uuid/random/time), so the SAME key always yields the
751	    SAME id across runs — required for the downstream P6 join on
752	    ``(claim_cluster_id, origin_cluster_id)``.
753	    """
754	    canonical = repr(tuple(normalized_key))
755	    digest = hashlib.sha1(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:_CLAIM_ID_HASH_LEN]
756	    return f"{_CLAIM_ID_PREFIX}{digest}"
757	
758	
759	def cluster_equivalent_claims(
760	    claims: list[AtomicClaim],
761	) -> dict[str, list[int]]:
762	    """Group EQUIVALENT atomic claims under one stable ``claim_cluster_id``.
763	
764	    Conservative-singleton safety: claims share a cluster ONLY when their
765	    ``normalized_key`` is EQUAL. Per-claim sentinel keys (unknown subject / raw text)
766	    are unique by construction, so such claims are always singletons — two distinct
767	    claims NEVER over-merge. MUTATES each claim's ``claim_cluster_id`` in place (the
768	    claims are this module's own objects, never the caller's evidence rows).
769	
770	    Returns ``{claim_cluster_id: [member indices into ``claims``]}``.
771	    """
772	    clusters: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
773	    for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
774	        cid = _claim_cluster_id(claim.normalized_key)
775	        claim.claim_cluster_id = cid
776	        clusters.setdefault(cid, []).append(idx)
777	    return clusters
778	
779	
780	# ── contradiction / refutation edges (recall-first) ───────────────────────────
781	
782	
783	def _cluster_id_for_evidence(claims: list[AtomicClaim]) -> dict[str, set[str]]:
784	    """Map evidence_id -> the set of claim_cluster_ids its claims belong to (fallback only)."""
785	    out: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
786	    for claim in claims:
787	        out.setdefault(claim.evidence_id, set()).add(claim.claim_cluster_id)
788	    return out
789	
790	
791	def _cluster_ids_by_subject(claims: list[AtomicClaim]) -> dict[tuple, set[str]]:
792	    """Map ``(evidence_id, subject)`` -> claim_cluster_ids.
793	
794	    Used to attach a contradiction edge ONLY to the clusters that share the edge's
795	    SUBJECT on an endpoint row — so when a row hosts several distinct atomic claims, a
796	    contradiction about one subject does NOT pull in the unrelated clusters of a
797	    DIFFERENT subject on the same row (Codex iter-1 P2). Subject is normalized
798	    (strip+lower) to match both the edge record's subject and the AtomicClaim subject.
799	    """
800	    out: dict[tuple, set[str]] = {}
801	    for claim in claims:
802	        sig = (claim.evidence_id, (claim.subject or "").strip().lower())
803	        out.setdefault(sig, set()).add(claim.claim_cluster_id)
804	    return out
805	
806	
807	def _edge_cluster_pair(
808	    subject: str,
809	    evidence_ids: tuple,
810	    cluster_by_subject: dict[tuple, set[str]],
811	    cluster_by_evid: dict[str, set[str]],
812	) -> tuple:
813	    """Sorted claim_cluster_ids of the SUBJECT-matching claims on the endpoint rows.
814	
815	    For each endpoint evidence row, attach the clusters whose claims share the edge's
816	    subject. If an endpoint has no subject-matching claim (e.g. an empty-subject
817	    semantic edge), fall back to that row's clusters so the edge is never lost
818	    (recall-first) — coarser only in that rare case, never the common multi-subject-row
819	    contamination (Codex iter-1 P2).
820	    """
821	    subj = (subject or "").strip().lower()
822	    ids: set[str] = set()
823	    for evid in evidence_ids:
824	        matched = cluster_by_subject.get((str(evid), subj), set())
825	        ids.update(matched if matched else cluster_by_evid.get(str(evid), set()))
826	    return tuple(sorted(ids))
827	
828	
829	def build_contradiction_edges(
830	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
831	    claims: list[AtomicClaim],
832	    *,
833	    domain: str | None = None,
834	    nli_judge: Optional[Callable[[str, str], tuple]] = None,
835	    numeric_extractor: Callable[..., list[ExtractedNumericClaim]] = extract_numeric_claims,
836	    qualitative_extractor: Callable[..., list[QualitativeAssertion]] = extract_qualitative_assertions,
837	) -> list[ContradictionEdge]:
838	    """Build contradiction / refutation edges over the atomic claims — RECALL-FIRST.
839	
840	    Reuses the three existing detectors as edge sources (used, not re-implemented):
841	      * numeric: ``detect_contradictions`` over ``extract_numeric_claims`` — a
842	        >threshold numeric disagreement on the same (subject, predicate, unit, dose).
843	      * qualitative: ``detect_qualitative_conflicts`` over
844	        ``extract_qualitative_assertions`` — present-vs-absent assertion-status
845	        conflicts + review flags (the no-number lethal-miss class).
846	      * semantic (OPTIONAL): only when an ``nli_judge`` is injected. Clusters rows by
847	        shared salient words, judges pairs, keeps ``contradict`` pairs. The judge is
848	        ``(claim_a, claim_b) -> (label, confidence)``; its per-pair fail-open (a judge
849	        ERROR skips THAT pair) is the only place an edge can be missed, and it is
850	        confined to the LLM path — the deterministic numeric + qualitative edges are
851	        never suppressed. NO judge is constructed here: off-mode / no-judge ⇒ no
852	        network, no spend (the production judge is wired by the caller, not this
853	        pure library).
854	
855	    Recall-first: we OVER-detect (coarse review flags included) and NEVER silently
856	    drop a real conflict. Endpoints are attached to their ``claim_cluster_id`` via the
857	    already-clustered ``claims`` so a downstream vote (P6) sees the conflict.
858	
859	    Returns ``list[ContradictionEdge]``, de-duplicated by
860	    (source, subject, predicate, sorted endpoint evidence_ids).
861	    """
862	    rows = list(rows or [])
863	    cluster_by_evid = _cluster_id_for_evidence(claims)
864	    cluster_by_subject = _cluster_ids_by_subject(claims)
865	    edges: list[ContradictionEdge] = []
866	    seen: set[tuple] = set()
867	
868	    def _add(source: str, subject: str, predicate: str,
869	             evidence_ids: list, severity: str) -> None:
870	        evid_tuple = tuple(sorted(str(e) for e in evidence_ids if str(e)))
871	        if len(evid_tuple) < 2:
872	            return  # an edge needs two distinct endpoints
873	        dedup_key = (source, subject, predicate, evid_tuple)
874	        if dedup_key in seen:
875	            return
876	        seen.add(dedup_key)
877	        edges.append(ContradictionEdge(
878	            source=source,
879	            subject=subject,
880	            predicate=predicate,
881	            evidence_ids=evid_tuple,
882	            claim_cluster_ids=_edge_cluster_pair(
883	                subject, evid_tuple, cluster_by_subject, cluster_by_evid
884	            ),
885	            severity=str(severity or "review"),
886	        ))
887	
888	    # 1. numeric contradictions (deterministic, recall-first within its threshold).
889	    try:
890	        numeric_claims = numeric_extractor(rows, domain) if domain is not None \
891	            else numeric_extractor(rows)
892	    except TypeError:
893	        numeric_claims = numeric_extractor(rows)
894	    # B9: thread the deterministic is_clinical signal so a non-clinical numeric
895	    # gap with unconfirmed scope is labeled possible_metric_mismatch, not a hard
896	    # contradiction. Clinical (default True) is byte-identical.
897	    from src.polaris_graph.domain.domain_signal import is_clinical_domain
898	    _is_clinical_cg = is_clinical_domain(domain, rows)
899	    for rec in detect_contradictions(numeric_claims, is_clinical=_is_clinical_cg):
900	        _add(
901	            "numeric",
902	            getattr(rec, "subject", ""),
903	            getattr(rec, "predicate", ""),
904	            [getattr(c, "evidence_id", "") for c in getattr(rec, "claims", [])],
905	            getattr(rec, "severity", "review"),
906	        )
907	
908	    # 2. qualitative conflicts (present-vs-absent + review flags, never silent-drop).
909	    try:
910	        qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows, domain) if domain is not None \
911	            else qualitative_extractor(rows)
912	    except TypeError:
913	        qual_assertions = qualitative_extractor(rows)
914	    for rec in detect_qualitative_conflicts(qual_assertions):
915	        _add(
916	            "qualitative",
917	            getattr(rec, "subject", ""),
918	            getattr(rec, "predicate", ""),
919	            [c.get("evidence_id", "") for c in getattr(rec, "claims", [])],
920	            getattr(rec, "severity", "review"),
921	        )
922	
923	    # 3. semantic NLI conflicts — ONLY when a judge is injected (no judge ⇒ no spend).
924	    if nli_judge is not None:
925	        clusters = cluster_candidate_rows(
926	            rows,
927	            min_overlap=_int_env(_ENV_NLI_MIN_OVERLAP, _DEFAULT_NLI_MIN_OVERLAP),
928	            max_rows=_int_env(_ENV_NLI_MAX_ROWS, _DEFAULT_NLI_MAX_ROWS),
929	        )
930	        if clusters:
931	            pairs = extract_pairs(
932	                clusters,
933	                max_pairs=_int_env(_ENV_NLI_MAX_PAIRS, _DEFAULT_NLI_MAX_PAIRS),
934	            )
935	            sem_records = detect_semantic_conflicts(
936	                pairs,
937	                nli_judge,
938	                min_confidence=_float_env(
939	                    _ENV_NLI_MIN_CONFIDENCE, _DEFAULT_NLI_MIN_CONFIDENCE
940	                ),
941	            )
942	            for rec in sem_records:
943	                _add(
944	                    "semantic",
945	                    getattr(rec, "subject", ""),
946	                    getattr(rec, "predicate", ""),
947	                    [c.get("evidence_id", "") for c in getattr(rec, "claims", [])],
948	                    getattr(rec, "severity", "review"),
949	                )
950	
951	    return edges
952	
953	
954	def build_claim_graph(
955	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
956	    *,
957	    domain: str | None = None,
958	    nli_judge: Optional[Callable[[str, str], tuple]] = None,
959	    numeric_extractor: Callable[..., list[ExtractedNumericClaim]] = extract_numeric_claims,
960	    qualitative_extractor: Callable[..., list[QualitativeAssertion]] = extract_qualitative_assertions,
961	) -> ClaimGraph:
962	    """End-to-end: extract atomic claims -> cluster equivalents -> build edges.
963	
964	    Pure orchestration over the building blocks above. The edge-source detectors +
965	    the optional NLI judge are dependency-injected so the whole graph builds offline
966	    with fakes / fixtures. No flag check here — the caller gates the invocation via
967	    ``claim_graph_enabled()`` (default-OFF), keeping this a pure library function.
968	    """
969	    claims = extract_atomic_claims(
970	        rows,
971	        domain=domain,
972	        numeric_extractor=numeric_extractor,
973	        qualitative_extractor=qualitative_extractor,
974	    )
975	    clusters = cluster_equivalent_claims(claims)
976	    edges = build_contradiction_edges(
977	        rows,
978	        claims,
979	        domain=domain,
980	        nli_judge=nli_judge,
981	        numeric_extractor=numeric_extractor,
982	        qualitative_extractor=qualitative_extractor,
983	    )
984	    return ClaimGraph(
985	        claims=claims,
986	        clusters=clusters,
987	        edges=edges,
988	        raw_row_count=len(list(rows or [])),
989	        distinct_cluster_count=len(clusters),

989	        distinct_cluster_count=len(clusters),
990	    )
991
